feedback.text: As a long-time consumer of digital entertainment, I strongly support the inclusion of video games in the scope of the Digital Fairness Act. The current model of digital ownership in gaming is deeply flawed and unfair to consumers. For example, I owned *Overwatch* and *Counter-Strike: Global Offensive (CS:GO)*two games that no longer exist in their original form. Overwatch was replaced by Overwatch 2, and CS:GO was forcibly updated to CS2. In the case of CS2, my PC could not run the new version, effectively making a game I paid for unplayable. This kind of forced obsolescence and lack of consumer choice is unacceptable. Consumers deserve the right to retain access to the products they purchase, especially when those products are digital and tied to online platforms. I urge the Commission to consider protections that ensure digital games remain accessible and playable, even after updates or publisher decisions. Thank you for taking this important step toward digital fairness.
